**Bedford** is a city in [Taylor County](/wiki/Taylor_County,_Iowa "Taylor County, Iowa"), [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"), United States. The population was 1,508 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-cen2020-3) It is the [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") of Taylor County.[[4]](#cite_note-GR6-4) [Lake of Three Fires State Park](/wiki/Lake_of_Three_Fires_State_Park "Lake of Three Fires State Park") is located several miles northeast of Bedford.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The community is believed to have gotten its name from Thomas J. Bedford, who was the first west bound [Pony Express](/wiki/Pony_Express "Pony Express") rider to deliver the mail on its final leg from [Benicia, California](/wiki/Benicia,_California "Benicia, California") to [Oakland, California](/wiki/Oakland,_California "Oakland, California") on April 23, 1861. Bedford is believed to have operated Bedfords Store and Trading Post in 1848 within the city limits. He moved to [California](/wiki/California "California") later that year, in the [California Gold Rush](/wiki/California_Gold_Rush "California Gold Rush").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The community was called "Grove" when a post office was established on June 30, 1855. On June 24, 1856 it was changed to Bedford. Other histories have noted possible other sources of the name:[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* [Bedford](/wiki/Bedford "Bedford") in England (possible original home of early settlers)
* [Bedford, Indiana](/wiki/Bedford,_Indiana "Bedford, Indiana") (reputed home of the surveyor)
* [Bedford, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Bedford,_Pennsylvania "Bedford, Pennsylvania") (reputed home of a "government engineer")

The community was formally established as the county seat of Taylor County by a legislative act in 1853. The town was formally laid out on lots sold on July 4, 1853 (which at the time identified the community as "Bedforde"). According to town legend, a barrel of whiskey was placed on the grounds and lots sold for $10 to $20 - although none of the lots were paid for at the time.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The present [Taylor County Courthouse](/wiki/Taylor_County_Courthouse_(Iowa) "Taylor County Courthouse (Iowa)") was built in 1893.

Bedford was served by a branch of the Burlington Northern Railroad (formerly Chicago, Burlington and Quincy Railroad or "CB&Q") that ran from Creston, Iowa to St. Joseph, Missouri This line was removed along with almost all the branches in southwestern Iowa and northwestern Missouri in the late 1970s to early 1980s[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Bedford is also served by Iowa Highway 148 which runs north and south into Missouri, and Iowa Highway 2 runs east and west and connects most of the county seats in the southern tier of Iowa counties. There is an excellent series of farm to market road system, mostly paved, as well.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/43/Bedford%2C_Iowa_City_hall.jpg/250px-Bedford%2C_Iowa_City_hall.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bedford,_Iowa_City_hall.jpg)

Bedford City Hall

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/BedfordIaLibrary.jpg/250px-BedfordIaLibrary.jpg)](/wiki/File:BedfordIaLibrary.jpg)

Bedford Carneige Library

Bedford is located along the East Fork of the [One Hundred and Two River](/wiki/One_Hundred_and_Two_River "One Hundred and Two River").

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 1.61 square miles (4.17 km2), of which 1.60 square miles (4.14 km2) is land and 0.01 square miles (0.03 km2) is water.[[10]](#cite_note-Gazetteer_files-10)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

According to the [Köppen Climate Classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_Climate_Classification "Köppen Climate Classification") system, Bedford has a [hot-summer humid continental climate](/wiki/Hot-summer_humid_continental_climate "Hot-summer humid continental climate"), abbreviated "Dfa" on climate maps.

The population of Bedford, Iowa from US census data

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/United_States_census "United States census") of 2020,[[14]](#cite_note-14) there were 1,508 people, 620 households, and 376 families residing in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 939.4 inhabitants per square mile (362.7/km2). There were 701 housing units at an average density of 436.7 per square mile (168.6/km2). The [racial](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_census "Race and ethnicity in the United States census") makeup of the city was 94.2% [White](/wiki/White_Americans "White Americans"), 0.1% [Black or African American](/wiki/African_Americans "African Americans"), 0.3% [Native American](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States"), 0.3% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans"), 0.0% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_Americans "Pacific Islander Americans"), 1.4% from other races and 3.8% from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") persons of any race comprised 1.5% of the population.

Of the 620 households, 28.9% of which had children under the age of 18 living with them, 47.4% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 7.4% were cohabitating couples, 29.5% had a female householder with no spouse or partner present and 15.6% had a male householder with no spouse or partner present. 39.4% of all households were non-families. 33.4% of all households were made up of individuals, 20.2% had someone living alone who was 65 years old or older.

The median age in the city was 39.0 years. 28.2% of the residents were under the age of 20; 5.2% were between the ages of 20 and 24; 23.0% were from 25 and 44; 20.2% were from 45 and 64; and 23.3% were 65 years of age or older. The gender makeup of the city was 47.9% male and 52.1% female.

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[15]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-15) of 2010, there were 1,440 people, 614 households, and 382 families living in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 900.0 inhabitants per square mile (347.5/km2). There were 730 housing units at an average density of 456.3 per square mile (176.2/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 97.4% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.5% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.1% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.3% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.8% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), and 1.0% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 1.3% of the population.

There were 614 households, of which 28.2% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 50.3% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 8.5% had a female householder with no husband present, 3.4% had a male householder with no wife present, and 37.8% were non-families. 33.1% of all households were made up of individuals, and 19.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.29 and the average family size was 2.91.

The median age in the city was 43.9 years. 24.1% of residents were under the age of 18; 6.8% were between the ages of 18 and 24; 20.6% were from 25 to 44; 25.4% were from 45 to 64; and 23.2% were 65 years of age or older. The gender makeup of the city was 47.2% male and 52.8% female.

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[16]](#cite_note-GR2-16) of 2000, there were 1,620 people, 691 households, and 422 families living in the city. The population density was 1,007.2 inhabitants per square mile (388.9/km2). There were 769 housing units at an average density of 478.1 per square mile (184.6/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 98.15% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.06% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.25% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.12% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.74% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 0.68% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 1.67% of the population.

There were 691 households, out of which 25.0% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 51.1% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 8.1% had a female householder with no husband present, and 38.9% were non-families. 33.1% of all households were made up of individuals, and 21.4% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.28 and the average family size was 2.95.

Age spread: 23.0% under the age of 18, 7.4% from 18 to 24, 23.2% from 25 to 44, 19.8% from 45 to 64, and 26.7% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 42 years. For every 100 females, there were 84.1 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 79.6 males.

The median income for a household in the city was $28,125, and the median income for a family was $34,943. Males had a median income of $27,788 versus $18,125 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $14,313. About 10.4% of families and 13.7% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 21.4% of those under age 18 and 12.2% of those age 65 or over.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bedford,_Iowa&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Education")]

It is within the [Bedford Community School District](/wiki/Bedford_Community_School_District "Bedford Community School District").[[17]](#cite_note-17)
